Boosting military spending beyond current levels may appease Trump temporarily, but it won’t solve the structural problem: Europe lacks strategic autonomy. What Europe truly needs is not more money, but more unity. The facts speak for themselves: USA spends about $997 billion annually on defense. China: $314 billion. Russia: $149 billion, even after a 38% increase in 2024. Europe + Canada (NATO members excluding USA): $539 billion. This means Europe alone already outspends Russia by more than 3.5 times, and nearly doubles China’s spending. So, the issue is not underfunding, it's duplication, fragmentation, and lack of coordination. If European countries moved toward a unified military structure, pooling resources, streamlining command, and coordinating logistics and procurement, they could achieve greater defense capability without increasing spending. Rather than chasing a 3% or 5% GDP target to satisfy Washington, Europe should build a common army and take responsibility for its own defense, while maintaining its alliance with the United States on equal footing, not as a dependent, but as a partner. That would placate not just Trump, but history.

Saying that Russia isn't the military threat NATO assumes might be accurate as they have shown their military to not be that much of a threat. The problem isn't so much Russia's current depleted abilities; but more the war mentality of Russia. We know that they will rebuild as much as they can and continue to create problems outside of their borders. That might seem a stretch, but for the last 100 years that's what Russia has always done. It's what they do, and that has not changed. Until the war mentality of Russia changes they will always present a threat. Again, it's what they do and have always done. The Russian government seems to validate their existence as a bulwark against the west, which they tell their people is a threat to their peace, supported by propaganda. The bottom line is that the west has to be strong to prevent Russia from overrunning their borders. If Russia thinks it can invade another nation and get away with it; history tells us that it will. It's what they do. With Ukraine, the problem for Russia is that they really did think they would be successful in less than a week. It's a manifestation of Putin's miscalculations. He miscalculated Ukraine's ability and determination. He miscalculated the west's addiction to Russian oil & gas. He miscalculated his ability to hold NATO back from his borders, due to his actions Finland & Sweden joined NATO. While trying to weaken NATO, he actually made it stronger. His greatest miscalculation however was his legacy. He thought this would give him more power, but it is draining his power instead. Now he's at the point where he's damned if he does and damned if he doesn't. What he thought would be very easy and make him great has left him worried about his own survival. Putin is screwed, but he did it to himself and Russia. He will have to answer to the Russians in the end, and the end doesn't look to be all that far away.
